delta.total_tokens: +20.2% (RPG uses more tokens due to story + achievement logic)

per_eval.notes: Both pass all assertions. RPG adds story framing and character dialogue without sacrificing teaching quality. RPG uses 22% more tokens.
Main fails story-related assertions (expected — feature doesn't exist). RPG passes all. Teaching content equally complete in both. RPG uses 13% more tokens.
Main cannot handle migration (expected). RPG correctly detects old format, adds RPG profile, retroactively unlocks 3 achievements, continues teaching. RPG uses 25% more tokens.

analyst_observations: TEACHING QUALITY PARITY: On the 'returning-student' eval (the fairest comparison), both branches score 7/7. The RPG layer does NOT degrade teaching quality.

STORY INTEGRATION: RPG branch's story elements stay within the 3-line limit and serve teaching goals. 小杰's wrong answers map to common SD interview anti-patterns (vertical scaling, 'MongoDB is web-scale').
TOKEN COST: RPG uses 13-25% more tokens across evals (mean +20%). This is the cost of story + achievement logic. Acceptable for the engagement benefit.

NON-DISCRIMINATING ASSERTIONS: 'teaching-quality', 'shows-chunk-map', 'feynman-gate-recall', 'feynman-gate-transfer' pass in both configurations — these test core teaching which is preserved in both branches.
DISCRIMINATING ASSERTIONS: 'uses-story-trigger', 'character-serves-learning', 'detects-old-format', 'retroactive-achievements' — these are RPG-specific features that differentiate the branches.

MIGRATION HANDLING: RPG branch's retroactive achievement logic correctly computes which achievements to unlock based on existing progress data. This is critical for users like this student who are already at Day 9.
